fields.description:
## Context / Problem

The outbound link differ compares in vendor shape. `outbound_links.py:30` imports
`_RELATION_TO_JIRA_LINK` from `adapters.jira.jira_fields`; `_existing_jira_links`
(`outbound_links.py:38-63`) reads the raw vendor `fields.issuelinks` list
(`{type:{name}, inwardIssue:{key}, outwardIssue:{key}}`) into a `(type_name, other_key)` set;
and `_diff_links` translates relation→`(jira_type, swap)` inline at `outbound_links.py:105`.
Epic bbf1 AC3 requires zero `adapters.jira` imports in core, and ADR 0035 §(d) requires that
core compare only canonical shapes (vendor payloads cross core opaquely, produced/consumed by
port calls at the boundaries — the same principle the sibling canonical field-diff story pins
for fields).

## Approach

Move both link translations onto the `SupportsLinks` capability (adapter-side, translation
only — neither member is parameterized on a shape a non-Jira backend lacks):

- `map_remote_links(remote_fields)` → the canonical link set: entries of
  `(relation, remote_key, opaque_vendor_type)`. The Jira implementation absorbs today's
  `_existing_jira_links` (`outbound_links.py:38-63`) including its direction-agnostic dedup
  (`:57-62`), translating vendor link-type names back to canonical relations via the adapter's
  own map; remote link types with no canonical relation are returned with `relation=None` so
  core continues to ignore them for matching and never removes them (today's semantics).
  The `opaque_vendor_type` passenger is carried untouched by core solely for the REMOVE
  payload's `type` key.
- `link_payload_for_relation(relation)` → `(vendor_type, swap)` — the emission translation,
  replacing the inline `_RELATION_TO_JIRA_LINK.get(relation)` at `outbound_links.py:105`.

Core `_diff_links`/`_diff_link_removals` (`outbound_links.py:66-135` and `:138-199`) then
compare local `ticket["deps"]` (`{target_id, relation, link_uuid}`) against the canonical set
in relation vocabulary. Emitted ADD/REMOVE payload keys are unchanged (ADD
`outbound_links.py:119-128`, REMOVE `:196-197`), so the link apply path is untouched.
`_RELATION_TO_JIRA_LINK` becomes adapter-internal. The AST neutrality gate in
`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py` is extended to assert zero
`adapters.jira` imports in `outbound_links.py`.

Rejected alternatives: (a) keeping the vendor link diff in core (violates §(d) and AC3);
(b) a port method that accepts the vendor issuelinks dict *and* the local deps and returns
the diff — that moves diffing onto the port, the exact violation class plan review blocked
on ticket 97f2's prior attempt (core owns diff; the port only translates).

## Scope

- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_backend.py (SupportsLinks: map_remote_links + link_payload_for_relation)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_links.py (canonical comparison; drop vendor import)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/backend.py (implement the two members)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/jira_fields.py (_RELATION_TO_JIRA_LINK becomes adapter-internal; reverse map)
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py (AST gate extended to outbound_links.py)
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ (link-diff behavior tests)

## Testing

- Happy path: a local dep missing remotely emits an ADD with today's exact payload keys and
  values (`type`, `to_key`, `relation`, `swap`, `link_uuid`); a dep present remotely (either
  direction) emits nothing.
- Edge/failure: unknown remote link types are ignored for matching and never removed;
  removals remain managed-ref gated (`outbound_links.py:138-199` semantics); REMOVE payload
  carries the original vendor type name via the opaque passenger.
- Gate: the extended AST case fails if `outbound_links.py` re-grows an `adapters.jira`
  import.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `outbound_links.py` contains NO `adapters.jira` import (module-level or
      function-nested) — verified by the extended AST case in
      `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py`.
- [ ] Link diffing compares canonical relations only; vendor link-type names appear in core
      solely as opaque payload passengers — verified by code review + the behavior tests in
      Testing (identical ADD/REMOVE payloads to today).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green; whole test tree collects with 0 errors.

## Plan-review remediation (drift re-review, G1G2 findings)

Two gaps are pinned here:

1. **`outbound_differ.py` is in scope.** `compute_outbound_mutations` (in `outbound_differ.py`)
   calls `_diff_links(ticket, jira_fields, binding_store)` at the update path. That call site
   must additionally pass the configured backend's `SupportsLinks` capability so `_diff_links`
   / `_diff_link_removals` can translate via `map_remote_links` / `link_payload_for_relation`.
   The backend is already in scope there (threaded for the field diff). So:
   `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_differ.py` is ADDED to Scope — it passes the
   `SupportsLinks` capability as the new 4th argument to `_diff_links`.

2. **The ADD dedup stays DIRECTION-AGNOSTIC.** Today `_existing_jira_links` inserts
   `(jira_type_name, other_key)` for the key present on EITHER `inwardIssue` OR `outwardIssue`,
   so a Jira link of a given type between the local issue and key K dedups a local dep to K
   REGARDLESS of which side K is on (verified: a local `blocks`→K dep against an INWARD Blocks
   link from K emits nothing today). The canonical set from `map_remote_links` carries the
   `opaque_vendor_type` passenger precisely so this is preserved: the core ADD dedup matches a
   local dep against a canonical entry on **`(opaque_vendor_type, remote_key)`** — the
   direction-agnostic pair equal to today's `(type_name, other_key)` — NOT on `(relation,
   remote_key)`. `link_payload_for_relation(relation)` yields the local dep's `(vendor_type,
   swap)`, and the dedup compares that `vendor_type` + target key against the canonical
   entries' `(opaque_vendor_type, remote_key)`. The `relation` field of a canonical entry
   drives ONLY the direction-SPECIFIC REMOVE managed-gate matching (mirroring today's
   `resolve_inbound_link`-based REMOVE). `map_remote_links` still applies today's
   direction-agnostic dedup internally (it may return one canonical entry per
   `(vendor_type, remote_key)`).

**Added acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] The ADD dedup is direction-agnostic: a local dep to K dedups against a remote link of the
      mapped vendor type between the local issue and K whether K is the inward or the outward
      side — verified by a held-out test (inward remote link vs outward local dep -> no ADD),
      matching today's behavior byte-for-byte.
- [ ] `outbound_differ.py` passes the `SupportsLinks` capability into `_diff_links`; the AST
      neutrality gate still passes for `outbound_differ.py` (the capability is the injected
      backend, not an `adapters.jira` import).

## Landed

Implemented and merged to `main` as Gerrit change 935 (LLM-Review +1, Verified +1).
